1→"""Tests for @json_command decorator, emit_json, emit_json_result, and --schema flag.""" 2→ 3→import json 4→from dataclasses import dataclass 5→from typing import Any 6→from unittest.mock import patch 7→ 8→import click 9→from click.testing import CliRunner 10→ 11→from erk_shared.agentclick.errors import AgentCliError 12→from erk_shared.agentclick.json_command import ( 13→ JsonCommandMeta, 14→ emit_json, 15→ emit_json_result, 16→ json_command, 17→) 18→ 19→# -- Helpers -- 20→ 21→ 22→def _make_test_command() -> click.Command: 23→ """Create a minimal Click command decorated with @json_command for testing.""" 24→ 25→ @json_command 26→ @click.command("test-cmd") 27→ def test_cmd(*, json_mode: bool) -> None: 28→ if json_mode: 29→ emit_json({"key": "value"}) 30→ return
... [489 lines omitted] ...
71→def test_catches_agent_cli_error() -> None: 72→ """AgentCliError is serialized as JSON when --json is active.""" 75→ @click.command("error-cmd") 76→ def error_cmd(*, json_mode: bool) -> None: 77→ raise AgentCliError("something went wrong", error_type="cli_error") 80→ result = runner.invoke(error_cmd, ["--json"]) 84→ assert data["error_type"] == "cli_error" 88→def test_preserves_error_type() -> None: 89→ """Custom error_type is preserved in JSON output.""" 92→ @click.command("typed-error-cmd") 93→ def typed_error_cmd(*, json_mode: bool) -> None: 94→ raise AgentCliError("auth failed", error_type="auth_required") 97→ result = runner.invoke(typed_error_cmd, ["--json"]) 101→ assert data["error_type"] == "auth_required" 102→ assert data["message"] == "auth failed" 118→def test_error_without_json_flag_uses_normal_handling() -> None: 119→ """Without --json, AgentCliError uses Click's normal error handling.""" 122→ @click.command("normal-error-cmd") 123→ def normal_error_cmd(*, json_mode: bool) -> None: 124→ raise AgentCliError("normal error", error_type="cli_error") 127→ result = runner.invoke(normal_error_cmd, []) 129→ # Should NOT be JSON — Click's normal error handling 130→ assert "normal error" in result.output 131→ # Verify it's not valid JSON (it's the styled Click error) 138→ except json.JSONDecodeError: 162→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 169→def test_json_input_unknown_keys_error() -> None: 170→ """Unknown keys produce JSON error.""" 185→ assert data["error_type"] == "invalid_json_input" 224→ assert data["error_type"] == "invalid_json_input" 229→ """Missing required field produces JSON error.""" 244→ assert data["error_type"] == "invalid_json_input" 356→ except json.JSONDecodeError: 449→ """--schema outputs a valid schema document with input/output/error schemas.""" 465→ assert "error_schema" in doc 466→ assert doc["error_schema"]["properties"]["success"]["const"] is False

1→"""Tests for JSON Schema generation from Click commands.""" 2→ 3→from dataclasses import dataclass 4→from typing import Any 5→ 6→import click 7→ 8→from erk_shared.agentclick.json_command import json_command 9→from erk_shared.agentclick.json_schema import ( 10→ ERROR_SCHEMA, 11→ build_schema_document, 12→ click_param_to_json_schema, 13→ command_input_schema, 14→ dataclass_to_json_schema, 15→ output_type_schema, 16→) 17→ 18→# -- click_param_to_json_schema tests -- 19→ 20→ 21→def test_string_param() -> None: 22→ param = click.Option(["--name"], type=click.STRING) 23→ schema = click_param_to_json_schema(param) 24→ assert schema["type"] == "string" 25→ 26→ 27→def test_int_param() -> None: 28→ param = click.Option(["--count"], type=click.INT) 29→ schema = click_param_to_json_schema(param) 30→ assert schema["type"] == "integer"

1→"""Tests for @json_command that depend on the erk CLI tree.""" 2→ 3→import types 4→import typing 5→ 6→import click 7→ 8→from erk.cli.cli import cli 9→ 10→# -- output_types validation test -- 11→ 12→ 13→def test_output_types_matches_return_annotation() -> None: 14→ """Every @json_command's output_types must match its return annotation.""" 15→ 16→ def _collect_json_commands(group: click.BaseCommand) -> list[click.BaseCommand]: 17→ """Recursively collect all commands with _json_command_meta.""" 18→ result = [] 19→ if hasattr(group, "_json_command_meta"): 20→ result.append(group) 21→ if isinstance(group, click.Group): 22→ for cmd in group.commands.values(): 23→ result.extend(_collect_json_commands(cmd)) 24→ return result 25→ 26→ def _unwrap_return_types(annotation: typing.Any) -> set[type]: 27→ """Decompose A | B unions into member types.""" 28→ origin = typing.get_origin(annotation) 29→ if origin is typing.Union or isinstance(annotation, types.UnionType): 30→ return set(typing.get_args(annotation))

1→"""Parity tests: every @mcp_exposed command is discovered and has correct schema.""" 2→ 3→from __future__ import annotations 4→ 5→import asyncio 6→ 7→from erk.cli.cli import cli 8→from erk_mcp.server import _build_json_command_tools, create_mcp 9→from erk_shared.agentclick.json_schema import command_input_schema 10→from erk_shared.agentclick.mcp_exposed import discover_mcp_commands 11→ 12→ 13→def test_every_mcp_exposed_command_is_registered_as_mcp_tool() -> None: 14→ """Every command with _mcp_meta in the Click tree appears as an MCP tool.""" 15→ discovered = discover_mcp_commands(cli, _parent_path=()) 16→ assert len(discovered) > 0, "No @mcp_exposed commands found — check CLI imports" 17→ 18→ server = create_mcp() 19→ tools = asyncio.run(server.list_tools()) 20→ tool_names = {t.name for t in tools} 21→ 22→ for _cmd, meta, _path in discovered: 23→ assert meta.name in tool_names, f"@mcp_exposed '{meta.name}' not registered as MCP tool" 24→ 25→ 26→def test_mcp_tool_schema_matches_click_derived_schema() -> None: 27→ """Each MCP tool's schema matches command_input_schema(cmd).""" 28→ discovered = discover_mcp_commands(cli, _parent_path=()) 29→ 30→ built_tools = _build_json_command_tools()
